patches.find: The earlier self-contradiction-with-PRED-TIER-3 framing remains true and is now housed in the cluster's vise subsection rather than carrying the verdict.
patches.replace: The earlier self-contradiction-with-PRED-TIER-3 framing remains true and is now housed in the cluster's vise subsection rather than carrying the verdict. Note that the underlying logical structure here is also structurally self-contradictory: combining the dome's own published κ (WIN-012), its own cited 2009 ΔB of order 5–15 nT, and its own cited SG measurement of 0 µGal forces a contradiction by the dome's arithmetic alone — the kernel of that taxonomic critique is correct. We classify the verdict 'Refuted by Data' rather than 'Self-Contradicted' because the load-bearing premise — the SG measurement of 0 µGal across the network — is an empirical observation that the dome itself accepts as validating data on its own claim, not a dome-derivation step. Self-contradiction is the structural pattern; the empirical SG null is what does the actual refuting. WIN-012 (the κ definition itself) remains 'Self-Contradicted' because its failure rests on dome-internal arithmetic alone, requiring no external data step. WIN-013 and WIN-014 — the eclipse-magnetic-vs-SG-null pair — are 'Refuted by Data' because each requires loading the corresponding empirical eclipse measurement.
patches.rationale: EXP-350: Appends verdict-taxonomy explainer paragraph to WIN-014.detail_verdict_text

patches.find: The right physical result on a globe — which Schumann (1952) derived theoretically and Balser & Wagner measured — is a fundamental near 7.83 Hz, in a spherical cavity.
patches.replace: The correct globe-physics prediction — which Schumann (1952) derived theoretically and Balser & Wagner measured — is a fundamental near 7.83 Hz, in a spherical cavity; the dome model has no equivalent derivation.
patches.rationale: EXP-375: Disambiguate Schumann sentence in WIN-001 to clarify dome lacks globe derivation

patches.find: at approximately 6, 18, and 30 Hz, recorded across his Colorado Springs Notes (1899–1900) and Wardenclyffe-era writings, reflecting an expectation of odd-harmonic spacing from a half-wave standing-wave geometry.

patches.rationale: EXP-375: Add secondary-source caveat to Tesla 6/18/30 Hz citation in WIN-001
